title: Free floating immunofluorescent staining protocol on mouse brain sections
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"58jns","text":"This protocol describes our multiplex fluorescent immunohistochemistry protocol used to identify pathological signatures in human iPSC-derived cells within thin, fixed mouse brain tissue section series’. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"32gs4","text":"We apply this workflow for post-mortem assessment of the inclusions within human iPSC-derived cells which have been transplanted into the living brain of athymic mice.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"1qcqv","text":"IMPORTANT: perform all antibody incubation steps and steps following in minimal light so as not to bleach signals prios to imaging","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: 30 um mouse brain sections were stored in anti-freeze solution at -20 °C until required. Remove samples from freezer and equilibrate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 - 00:20:00 Pour sections into a well insert in a 6-well plate to separate storage solution from section Move the well insert to another well containing approximately 6 mL of 1x PBS. Wash at least 5x with 1x PBS for 00:05:00 each on an orbital shaker using low speed at Room temperature
Incubate the sections in 10mM sodium citrate buffer (pH9.0) for 00:30:00 . Let it cool to Room temperature Rinse the sections 3x 00:05:00 each in 1X PBS
Weigh NaBH 4 to make 0.1~0.5% in 1X PBS, made fresh Move the insert with sections into the fresh-made solution for 00:30:00 at Room temperature Wash 2x 00:05:00 in 1X PBS
Incubate sections in normal donkey serum IF blocking buffer 02:00:00 Room temperature on shaker 60 rpm

Make primary antibody cocktails in blocking buffer Prepare ~ 300 µL per sample of primary antibody solution consisting of selected primaryantibody (diluted appropriately) in home-made normal donkey serum IF blockingbuffer Transfer sections from well insert into wells of black porcelain spot plate containingprimary antibody solution to bind to the antigen(s) of interest Place the plate on a rotating mixer using low speed (speed 7 rpm) and incubate 72:00:00 at 4 °C (or 3X night/ over weekend)

The following day, pour sections into a well insert in a 6-well plate to separatesections from primary antibody solution. Wash sections 3 times with 1x PBST at Room temperature . Note: 00:00:30 for the first two rinses, 3x 00:10:00 for additional washing Prepare 300 µL per sample of secondary antibody solution consisting of appropriatesecondary antibody + Hoechst 33342 (diluted accordingly) in blocking buffer (shieldsolution from light) Transfer sections into the black porcelain spot plate containing 300 µL secondaryantibody cocktail Incubate for 02:00:00 at Room temperature on orbital shaker using low speed (shield solution from light). Pour sections into a well-insert in a 6-well plate containing 1X PBST to separate sections from the secondary antibody solution Continuing to shield samples from light, wash 3 times with 1x PBS for 00:05:00 at Room temperature

Pour sections into a glass petri dish Submerge a glass slide into the 1x PBS and use a fine paintbrush to coax the sections towards the slide Gently tap the sections onto the slide, making sure there are no wrinkles or folds Repeat until all sections are mounted onto the slide(s)

After sections are dried onto the slide(s), about 00:15:00 at Room temperature or until sections look opaque (remember to shield slides from light), apply an appropriate aqueous mounting medium(hardening or non-hardening). Antifading (DAKO Fluorescence Mounting Mediumis preferred if using a fluorescent conjugated secondary antibody Using tweezers, place a coverslip on top of the medium. Cover with filter paper and press down firmly to remove excess mounting medium Image sections using an appropriate microscope. Store in a dark slide box at 4 °C
creator: courtney.wright Wright
